title: Kendrick Lamar Becomes First Rapper to Win Record of the Year Twice
text: At the 2026 Grammy Awards, Kendrick Lamar and SZA won Record of the Year for their collaboration "luther," marking a historic achievement for the rapper. Lamar became the first male artist to consecutively win the Record of the Year award and the first rapper ever to win it twice. He previously won the award last year for "Not Like Us." This victory also solidified his status as the most-decorated rapper in Grammy history, surpassing Jay-Z's previous record of 25 Grammys with a total of 27 awards. Cher presented the award but experienced an on-stage mishap, initially walking off before returning to announce the winner. She mistakenly referenced Luther Vandross during the announcement—a connection to the track itself, which samples a classic song featuring the late R&B legend. During their acceptance speech, Lamar and SZA acknowledged the song's heritage. Lamar stated: "Luther Vandross. This is special for me...one of my favorite artists of all time." He emphasized how meaningful it was to receive clearance to build upon Vandross's legacy. SZA used the moment to address broader concerns, urging listeners to maintain hope during challenging times. The award beat out entries from Bad Bunny, Sabrina Carpenter, Doechii, Billie Eilish, Lady Gaga, Chappell Roan, and Rosé and Bruno Mars.
